questions.question_html: Using tally marks which one of the following represents the number five?

options.explanation: Using tally marks  represents the number five.

questions.question_html: A tabular form of data is also a pictorial representation of the data.

options.explanation:
False,
A tabular form of data represents data in numerals whereas the pictorial representation of the data represents data in picture form.

questions.question_html: A pictograph represents data in the form of pictures of objects or parts of objects.

The blood groups of 25 students are recorded as under:
A, B, O, A, AB, O, A, O, B, A, O, B, A, AB, AB, A, A, B, B, O, B, AB, O, A, B.
Arrange the information in a table using tally marks.
questions.solution_html:
The given data can be represented in a table using tally marks as; 

Blood group  Tally marks Number of students
A {tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex} {tex}|||{/tex} 8
B {tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex} {tex}||{/tex} 7
O {tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex} {tex}|{/tex} 6
AB {tex}||||{/tex} 4

questions.question_html:
In a school, there are five sections of Class VI. The number of students in each section is given below:

Section A B C D E
Number of students 40 44 42 36 32

Represent the above data using a bar graph.
questions.solution_html:
First of all, draw a horizontal line and a vertical line.
Choose a suitable scale along the vertical line, say 1 unit length = 4 students.
On the horizontal line, write sections A, B, C, D and E.
Draw vertical bars on the respective section showing the number of students in that section.
Hence, all the data is represented in the bar diagram below.

questions.question_html:
The number of visitors in a science exhibition on different days of a week is shown below:

Day Number of Visitors  = 100 visitors
Monday
Tuesday
Wednesday
Thursday
Friday
Saturday

Look at the above pictograph and answer the following questions: 

  1. What is the total number of visitors from Monday to Saturday?
  2. On which day was the number of visitors maximum? What was their total number?
  3.  On which day was the number of visitors minimum?
  4. On which day was the number of visitors same as the number of visitors on two days taken together?
questions.solution_html:
Number of visitors on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday was 550, 700, 1000, 900, 900, and 1250 respectively.

  1. The total number of visitors from Monday to Saturday = 550 + 700 + 1000 + 900 + 900 + 1250 = 5300.
  2. The number of visitors was maximum on Saturday and the number was 1250.
  3. The number of visitors was minimum on Monday and the number was 550.
  4. The number of visitors on Saturday was 1250 which was equal to the total number of visitors on Monday (550) and Tuesday (700).

questions.question_html: Using tally marks, which one of the following represents the number eight:

options.explanation:
Using tally marks, the data in this option is,
5 + 3 = 8

questions.question_html:
The marks (out of 10) obtained by 28 students in a Mathematics test are listed as below:
8, 1, 2, 6, 5, 5, 5, 0, 1, 9, 7, 8, 0, 5, 8, 3, 0, 8, 10, 10, 3, 4, 8, 7, 8, 9, 2, 0
The number of students who obtained marks more than or equal to 5 is

options.explanation:
Arranging the marks obtained in increasing order,
0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 4, 5, 5, 5, 5, 6, 7, 7, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 9, 9, 10, 10.
The number of students who obtained marks more than or equal to 5 is 17.

questions.question_html:
The marks (out of 10) obtained by 28 students in a Mathematics test are listed as below:
8, 1, 2, 6, 5, 5, 5, 0, 1, 9, 7, 8, 0, 5, 8, 3, 0, 8, 10, 10, 3, 4, 8, 7, 8, 9, 2, 0
the number of students who scored marks less than 4 is

options.explanation:
Arrange the marks (out of 10) obtained in increasing order,
0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 4, 5, 5, 5, 5, 6, 7, 7, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 9, 9, 10, 10.
The number of students who scored marks less than 4 is 10.

questions.question_html:
The choices of the fruits of 42 students in a class are as follows:
A, O, B, M, A, G, B, G, A, G,
B, M, A, G, M, A, B, G, M, B,
A, O, M, O, G, B, O, M, G, A,
A, B, M, O, M, G, B, A, M, O, M, O,
where A, B, G, M and O stand for the fruits Apple, Banana, Grapes, Mango and Orange respectively. Which two fruits are liked by an equal number of students?

options.explanation: By observing the choices of the fruits of 42 students in a class, Banana (B) and Grapes(G) are liked by an equal number of students i.e. 8 students each.

questions.question_html:
The choices of the fruits of 42 students in a class are as follows:
A, O, B, M, A, G, B, G, A, G,
B, M, A, G, M, A, B, G, M, B,
A, O, M, O, G, B, O, M, G, A,
A, B, M, O, M, G, B, A, M, O, M, O,
where A, B, G, M and O stand for the fruits Apple, Banana, Grapes, Mango and Orange respectively.
which fruit is liked by most of the students?

options.explanation: Mango(M) is liked by most of the students.

questions.question_html: In a bar graph, the width of bars may be unequal.

options.explanation:
False.
We know that, In a bar graph, bars of uniform width are drawn horizontally or vertically with equal spacing between them.

questions.question_html: In a bar graph, bars of uniform width are drawn vertically only.

options.explanation:
False.
We know that, In a bar graph, bars of uniform width are drawn horizontally or vertically with equal spacing between them.

questions.question_html: In a bar graph, the gap between two consecutive bars may not be the same

options.explanation:
False.
We know that in a bar graph, bars of uniform width are drawn horizontally or vertically with equal spacing between them.

questions.question_html: In a bar graph, each bar (rectangle) represents only one value of the numerical data.

options.explanation:
True.
Follows from definition of Bar graph.

questions.question_html: To represent the population of different towns using bar graph, it is convenient to take one unit length to represent one person.

options.explanation:
False.
Population of towns are large numbers, then we may need a bigger scale.

questions.question_html: Pictographs and bar graphs are pictorial representations of the numerical data.

options.explanation:
True.
By definition, pictographs and bar graphs are representations of the numerical data.

questions.question_html: An observation occurring five times in a data is recorded as | | | | |, using tally marks.

options.explanation:
False.
We know that an observation occurring five times in a data is recorded as  using tally marks.

questions.question_html: In a pictograph, if a symbol represents 50 books in a library shelf, then the symbol  represents 25 books.

options.explanation:
True.
Full picture represents 50 books then half picture represents 25 books.

questions.question_html: ________ is a collection of numbers gathered to give some meaningful information.
The data can be arranged in a tabular form using ________ marks.
A ________ represents data through pictures of objects.
In a bar graph, ________ can be drawn horizontally and vertically.
In a bar graph, bars of ________ width can be drawn horizontally or vertically with ________ spacing between them.
An observation occurring seven times in a data is represented as ________ using tally marks.
In a pictograph, if a symbol  represents 20 flowers in a basket then  stands for ________ flowers.
On the scale of 1 unit length = 10 crore, the bar of length 6 units will represent ________ crore and of ________ units will represent 75 crore.
questions.solution_html:
Given that, 1 – unit length = 10 crore,
So, bar of length 6 unit = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 60 crore
Then, the number of units will represent 75 crore = 75/10
= 7.5 units
Therefore, On the scale of 1-unit length = 10 crore, the bar of length 6 units will represent 60 crore and of 7.5 units will represent 75 crore.
Hence, 60 and 7.5

Grades Tally marks Number of Students

1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 3 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 4, 1, 3, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2, 3, 2, 1, 1
Find the number of families having two or more, two-wheelers.
questions.solution_html:
Given that, the number of two wheelers owned individually by each of 50 families are,
1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 3, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 4, 1, 3, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2, 3, 2, 1, 1

Number of two wheelers Tally marks
0 {tex}|||{/tex}
1 {tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex}{tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex}{tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex}{tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex}{tex}\cancel{||||}{/tex}{tex}|||{/tex}

Number of families having two or more, two-wheelers is 19.

questions.question_html:

questions.question_html:
Following pictograph represents some surnames of people listed in the telephone directory of a city

Surname Number of People  = 100 People
Khan
Patel
Rao
Roy
Saikia
Singh

Observe the pictograph and answer the following questions:

  1. How many people have the surname ‘Roy’?
  2. Which surname appears the maximum number of times in the telephone directory?
  3. Which surname appears the least number of times in the directory?
  4. Which two surnames appear an equal number of times?
questions.solution_html:
From the pictogarph, we have, 

Surname  Number of people
Khan 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 + 50 = 350
Patel 5 {tex}\times{/tex}100 = 500
Rao 4 {tex}\times{/tex}100 = 400
Roy 4 {tex}\times{/tex}100 = 400
Saikia 2 {tex}\times{/tex}100 = 200
Singh 3 {tex}\times{/tex}100 = 300
  1. 400 people have surname 'Roy' in the telephone directory.
  2. The surname 'Patel' appears the maximum number of times in the telephone directory.
  3. The surname 'Saikia' appears the least number of times in the telephone directory.
  4. The surnames 'Rao' and 'Roy' appear an equal number of times.

questions.question_html:
Students of Class VI in a school were given a task to count the number of articles made of different materials in the school. The information collected by them is represented as follows:

Material Articles  = 20 articles
Wood
Glass
Metal 
Rubber
Plastic

Observe the pictograph and answer the following questions:

  1. Which material is used in a maximum number of articles?
  2. Which material is used in a minimum number of articles?
  3. Which material is used in exactly half the number of articles as those made up of metal?
  4. What is the total number of articles counted by the students?
questions.solution_html:
From the pictograph, we observe that, 

Material used Number of articles
Wood 20 + 10 = 30
Glass 20
Metal 20 + 20 + 10 = 50
Rubber 20 + 5 = 25
Plastic 20 + 15 = 35
Total 160
  1. Metal is used in a maximum number of articles.
  2. Glass is used in a minimum number of articles.
  3. Rubber is used in exactly half the number of articles as those made up of metal.
  4. The total number of the articles counted by the students is 160.

questions.question_html:
The number of scouts in a school is depicted by the following pictograph:

Class Number of Scout  = 10 scouts
VI
VII
VIII 
IX
X

Observe the pictograph and answer the following questions:

  1. Which class has the minimum number of scouts?
  2. Which class has the maximum number of scouts?
  3. How many scouts are there in Class VI?
  4. Which class has exactly four times the scouts as that of Class X?
  5. What is the total number of scouts in the Classes VI to X?
questions.solution_html:
From the given pictograph, we observe that, 

Class Number of scouts
VI 4 {tex}\times{/tex}10 = 40
VII 2 {tex}\times{/tex}10 = 20
VIII 6 {tex}\times{/tex}10 = 60
IX 3 {tex}\times{/tex}10 = 30
X 1 {tex}\times{/tex}10 = 10
Total 160
  1. Class X has a minimum number of scouts
  2. Class VIII has a maximum number of scouts.
  3. There are 40 scouts in Class VI.
  4. Class VI has exactly four times the scouts as that of Class X.
  5. The total number of scouts in the Classes VI to X is 160.

questions.question_html:
A survey was carried out in a certain school to find out the popular school subjects among students of Classes VI to VIII. The data in this regard is displayed as pictograph given below:

Subject Number of Students = 50 Students
Hindi
English
Mathematics 
Science
Social Studies
  1. Which subject is most popular among the students?
  2. How many students like Mathematics?
  3. Find the number of students who like subjects other than Mathematics and Science.
questions.solution_html:
From the pictograph table, we have, 

Subject Number of Students
Hindi 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 = 200
English 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 = 150
Mathematics 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 + 25 = 175
Science 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 = 100
Social Studies 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 + 25 = 75
Total 700
  1. Hindi is the most popular subject among students.
  2. 175 students like Mathematics.
  3. Total 200 + 150 + 75 = 425 students are there who like subjects other than Mathematics and science.

questions.question_html:
The following pictograph depicts the information about the areas in sq. km (to nearest hundred) of some districts of Chhattisgarh State:

District Area (in km2) =1000sqkm
Raigarh
Rajnandgaon
Koria
Mahasamund
Kabirdham
Jashpur
  1. What is the area of Koria district?
  2. Which two districts have the same area?
  3. How many districts have area more than 5000 square kilometres?
questions.solution_html:
From the given pictograph, we have, 

District Area (in km2)
Raigarh 6 {tex}\times{/tex}1000 + 500 = 6500
Rajnandgaon 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 1000 = 7000
Koria 6 {tex}\times{/tex}1000 = 6000
Mahasamund 5 {tex}\times{/tex}1000 = 5000
Kabirdham 4 {tex}\times{/tex}1000 = 4000
Jashpur 6 {tex}\times{/tex}1000 + 500 = 6500
  1. The area of Koria district is 6000 sq km.
  2. Raigarh and Jashpur districts have the same area of 6500 sq km.
  3. There are 4 districts that have area more than 5000 square kilometres and they are Raigarh, Rajnandgaon, Koria and Jashpur.

questions.question_html:
The following bar graph shows the number of houses (out of 100) in a town using different types of fuel for cooking.
Read the bar graph and answer the following questions: Scale: 1 unit length = 5 houses.

  1. Which fuel is used in maximum number of houses?
  2. How many houses are using coal as fuel?
  3. Suppose that the total number of houses in the town is 1 lakh. From the above graph estimate the number of houses using electricity.
questions.solution_html:
1. LPG is used in a maximum number of houses.
  2. 10 houses are using coal as fuel.
  3. Since there are 5 out of 100houses using electricity. Therefore, out of 1 lakh, the electricity will be used by {tex}\frac{5}{100} \times 100000{/tex} = 5000 houses.

questions.question_html:
The following bar graph represents the data for different sizes of shoes worn by the students in a school. Read the graph and answer the following questions.
Scale : 1 unit length = 50 students

  1. Find the number of students whose shoe sizes have been collected.
  2. What is the number of students wearing shoe size 6?
  3. What are the different sizes of the shoes worn by the students?
  4. Which shoe size is worn by the maximum number of students?
  5. Which shoe size is worn by a minimum number of students?
  6. State whether true or false:
    The total number of students wearing shoe sizes 5 and 8 is the same as the number of students wearing shoe size 6.
questions.solution_html:
From the  given bar graph, we have,

  1. The total number of students whose shoe sizes have been collected = 250 + 200 + 300 + 400 + 150 = 1300
  2. The number of students wearing shoe size 6 is 300.
  3. The different sizes of the shoes worn by the students are 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8
  4. The shoe size 7 is worn by the maximum number of students.
  5. The shoe size 8 is worn by the minimum number of students.
  6. False: Since the number of students wearing shoe sizes 5 and 8 is 350 and the number of students wearing shoe size 6 is 300.

questions.question_html:
The following graph gives information about the number of railway tickets sold for different cities on a railway ticket counter between 6.00 am to 10.00 am. Read the bar graph and answer the following questions:

  1. How many tickets were sold in all?
  2. For which city was the maximum number of tickets sold?
  3. For which city was the minimum number of tickets sold?
  4. Name the cities for which the number of tickets sold is more than 20.
  5. Fill in the blanks:
    Number of tickets sold for Delhi and Jaipur together exceeds the total number of tickets sold for Patna and Chennai by ________.
questions.solution_html:
1. Number of all ticket sold = 80 + 50 + 100 + 20 + 45 = 295
  2. For Delhi maximum number of tickets were sold.
  3. For Chennai minimum number of tickets were sold.
  4. Cities are Patna, Jaipur, Delhi and Guwahati, where more 20 tickets are sold.
  5. From the bar graph, Number of tickets sold for different cities are,
    Patna = 80
    Jaipur = 50
    Delhi = 100
    Chennai = 20 and
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} The number of tickets sold for Delhi and Jaipur together = 100 + 50 = 150
    The number of tickets sold for Patna and Chennai together = 80 + 20 = 100
    Now, 150 - 100 = 50
    Hence, The number of tickets sold for Delhi and Jaipur together exceeds the total number of tickets sold for Patna and Chennai by 50.

questions.question_html:
The bar graph given below represents approximate length (in kilometres) of some National Highways in India. Study the bar graph and answer the following questions:
Scale : 1 unit length = 100km

  1. Which National Highway (N.H.) is the longest among the above?
  2. Which National Highway is the shortest among the above?
  3. What is the length of National Highway 9?
  4. Length of which National Highway is about three times the National Highway 10?
questions.solution_html:
From the give bar graph, we conclude that, 

  1. The longest National Highway is N.H. 2.
  2. The shortest National Highway is N.H. 10.
  3. The length of National Highway 9 is 900 km.
  4. Length of National Highway 10 = 450 km.
    Length of National Highway 8 = 1400 km.
    So, the length of National Highway 8 is about three times the National Highway 10.

questions.question_html:
The bar graph given below represents the circulation of newspapers in different languages in a town. Study the bar graph and answer the following question:
Scale : 1 unit length = 200 Newspapers

  1. What is the circulation of the English newspaper?
  2. Name the two languages in which the circulation of the newspaper is the same.
  3. By how much is the circulation of newspapers in Hindi more than the newspaper in Bengali?
questions.solution_html:
From the given bar graph, we conculde, 

  1. The circulation of the English newspaper is 1000.
  2. In Bengali and Marathi languages the circulation of the newspaper is the same.
  3. The circulation of newspapers in Hindi is more than the newspaper in Bengali by 1400 - 600 = 800.

questions.question_html:
Read the bar graph given below and answer the following questions:
Scale : 1 unit = 50 students

  1. What information is given by the bar graph?
  2. In which year is the number of students maximum?
  3. In which year is the number of students twice as that of 2001-02?
  4. In which year did the number of students decrease as compared to the previous year?
  5. In which year is the increase in number of students maximum as compared to the previous year?
questions.solution_html:
1. The given bar graph shows the number of students in different academic years.
  2. In the year 2005 - 06, the number of students is maximum.
  3. Since the number of students in 2001 - 02 is 150 and the number of students in 2004 - 05 is 300 So, in the year 2004 - 05, the number of students is twice as that of 2001 - 02.
  4. The year is 2003 - 04 in which the number of students decreased as compared to the previous year.
  5. The year is 2004 - 05 in which the increase in the number of students is maximum as compared to the previous year.

questions.question_html:
Number of mobile phone users in various age groups in a city is listed below:

Age group (in years) Number of mobile users

questions.question_html:
In a botanical garden, the number of different types of plants are found as follows:

Type of the plant Number of plants
Herb 50
Shrub 60
Creeper 20
Climber 45
Tree 95

  1. Which type of plant is maximum in number in the garden?
  2. Which type of plant is minimum in number in the garden?
questions.solution_html:
The given information is shown in the bar graph drawn below where 1 scale unit = 10.

From the graph, we have,
1. Shrub.
2. Creeper.
